title: Black Hole
definition: A region of spacetime where gravity is so intense that nothing, not even light, can escape.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A mysterious and powerful cosmic object that has such a strong gravitational pull that not even light can get out.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A highly compact celestial entity arising from the collapse of massive stars, whose immense gravity creates an event horizon beyond which escape is impossible.
difficulty_levels.expert: An astrophysical phenomenon occurring when a massive star collapses, forming a region with such extreme gravitational force that not even light can escape, posing challenges to our understanding of physics and the universe's structure.

extra_fields.insight: The enigmatic, invisible gatekeepers of the cosmos, challenging our comprehension of space, time, and matter.
